preflight.note: Created before implementation work starts. Hardens RepoPact/validate_system.py so README<->work-item.json drift fails the audit instead of passing silently, and makes the audit a commit/push gate.

acceptance_criteria.text: validate_system.py fails when a README acceptance-criterion checkbox does not match the work-item.json criterion state (satisfied must be [x], pending must be [ ]) or when a criterion in the manifest has no checkbox line in the README.
validate_system.py fails when a satisfied criterion's evidence id is not referenced in the work item's README evidence log, so 'satisfied' always has a narrated, traceable record.

validate_system.py enforces the decision 0011 schema-migration invariants: CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION in database.ts equals the highest contiguous migration step, the version guards are contiguous from 0, and every shipped version has an allocation-table row in decision 0011.

A versioned .githooks/ gate runs the audit on every commit and the audit plus the test suite on every push, wired via core.hooksPath with a documented one-line setup, so drift cannot reach the branch by relying on memory.

A per-criterion closeout definition-of-done checklist is recorded in work/README.md, and the pre-commit hook emits a soft (non-blocking) reminder when backend source changes without a CHANGELOG entry, covering the judgment-laden steps that cannot be fully automated.
